segments.text: hey everybody welcome to this video on
five flask issues to avoid I'm your host
nafal Islam a developer Advocate at Sono
so I'm not going to waste too much time
flask is a python web framework that I'm
sure everybody knows about and we are
going to dive right into five issues
that I think a lot of people should
avoid the first one is that in a lot of
projects there's just no structure and
you should add structure because it
allows you to grow your application in
complexity so here's an example of how
you can add structure which is to use
the factory pattern to run your flask
app or to create your flask app one
example of how to add structure is to
use the factory pattern in your flask
application so you use a create app
method and in this app method you can of
course add routes but that's not what
you really do this is just an example uh
you can add routes and what's more
important is that you can add
configuration to it so here is an
example of a configuration file you you
can see that we have a base class of
config that inherits from object and we
say that testing is equal to false and
later on in production config we set the
database URI in development we set a
different database U URI we can say the
testing is equal to True whereas over
here testing is equal to false so very
typical python classes can be used to
configure your application and you can
have configuration in one place it's
also super easy to add that
configuration so here's an example if
config type is Dev we are going to go go
to app.config from object and we can say
that configs DOD development config
which is in a separate file so that's
one way in which you can add structure
to your application there are more ways
such as adding blueprints so here for
example you can create your app and you
can register blueprints which are kind
of like sub applications within your
flask application so when you're
building up flask applications it's a
good idea to follow this method so that
you have some structure in your
application because often times flask
applications can turn into very big one
file applications and it's important to
add structure early on now let's talk
about something that isn't really
particular to flask but I have seen a
lot of in flask applications which is
that there is no password hasher or
rather there is no password hashing uh
to make sure that your passwords are
secure and in order to do this you can
add it quite easily you can install fask
bcrypt and you can add bcrypt to your
flask application and once you have you
can just use flask decrypt to generate
your hash password and you can also use
it to check password okay so we're at
number three and this is something
that's not particular to flas but there
are a lot of people that use raw SQL in
flas queries now I can understand using
a raw SQL query or or something of that
nature to quickly test out a prototype
application but it's probably not not a
good idea because you are vulnerable to
SQL injections here's an example you get
your username from your requests it's
not sanitized you get this information
into your database and now the hacker
can basically inject any kind of SQL
into the database which isn't good so
make sure to use something like an orm
or a lightweight library that abstracts
over your SQL connection okay this one
is very particular to flask and that is
not using flask extensions here's
example of flask login which is one of
its most used extensions which allows
you to basically have requirements on
accessing routes for example here on
logout login is required on a protected
route login is required so please use
these extensions from flask to better
enhance the way that your applications
are written and lastly this is something
that I see a lot in a lot of code bases
which is that there is no error handling
what often happens is that errors are
just handled on the route itself using
file statements and often times you
don't really need to you can instead
designate error handlers to make sure
that you get the right error handling
for each particular error code so here
you have a 404 Handler which is a not
found and then you have a 500 Handler
which is an internal error this allows
you to have a lot more control over the
way your information is presented to
your users and it also allows you to
have a lot more control control over how
your errors are handled internally so
That's all folks those are five quick
ways in which you can rapidly and quite
radically improve your flask
applications thank you for watching and
I hope to see you again in the next one
